rulings.Case Title: ARCHITECTURAL METAL INC. VS. THOMPSON BUILDERS CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Enforce The Settlement Agreement And Enter Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Friday, April 25, 2025, Line 2. DEFENDANT THOMPSON BUILDERS's Motion To Enforce The Settlement Agreement And Enter Judgment. Before the court is Thompson Builders Corporation's Motion To Enforce The Settlement Agreement And Enter Judgment. Hearing required. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: GARY N. NEIL ET AL VS. BAMBOO INSURANCE SERVICES, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order To Compel Plaintiffs Gary N. Neil, Angela Noriega (Neil), And Gary A. Neil Compliance With Court'S Order And For Monetary And Terminating Sanctions; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Friday, April 25, 2025, Line 5. 1 - DEFENDANT GOOSEHEAD INSURANCE AGENCY, LLC.'s Motion For An Order To Compel Plaintiffs Gary N. Neil, Angela Noriega (Neil), And Gary A. Neil Compliance With Court'S Order And For Monetary And Terminating Sanctions. Defendant Goosehead Insurance Agency, LLC's motion to compel compliance with a court order is DENIED as moot, but the request for sanctions is GRANTED. Defendant's motion is now moot as to the discovery requests at issue. However, sanctions are still appropriate given Defendant was forced to make this motion. As Defendant correctly points out: "The court may award sanctions under the Discovery Act in favor of a party who files a motion to compel discovery, even though no opposition to the motion was filed, or opposition to the motion was withdrawn, or the requested discovery was provided to the moving party after the motion was filed." (CRC 3.1348(a).) Plaintiffs' excuse that a senior legal assistant was dealing with a death in the family is not enough to evade the effects of disobeying a court order. The responsibility of complying with a court order is on counsel of record. Plaintiffs provide no authority that a meet and confer attempt is required to compel compliance with a court order, or request sanctions for the abuse of the discovery process under CCP Section 2023.030. Furthermore, regardless of the reason for the delay, Defendant was forced to make this motion due to Plaintiffs' noncompliance. The record does not show Plaintiff acted with substantial justification with respect to this discovery. Plaintiff and counsel, jointly and severally, shall pay $1,760 to Defendant in sanctions. Payment should be made no later than 10 court days from notice of this order. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the moving party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: GARY N. NEIL ET AL VS. BAMBOO INSURANCE SERVICES, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order To Compel Plaintiffs Gary N. Neil, Angela Noriega (Neil), And Gary A. Neil To Respond To Goosehead'S Amended Requests For Admissions, Form Interrogatories, Set Two, And Request For Sanctions; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Friday, April 25, 2025, Line 5. 2 - DEFENDANT GOOSEHEAD INSURANCE AGENCY, LLC.'s Motion For An Order To Compel Plaintiffs Gary N. Neil, Angela Noriega (Neil), And Gary A. Neil To Respond To Goosehead'S Amended Requests For Admissions, Form Interrogatories, Set Two, And Request For Sanctions. Defendant Goosehead Insurance Agency, LLC's motion to compel responses to Amended Requests for Admission and Form Interrogatories, Set Two, is GRANTED. Plaintiffs filed a statement of non-opposition to the motion. The requests at issue are reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence. (CCP Section 2017.010.) Plaintiffs provided no responses, and thus no meet and confer process was required. Also, all non-privilege-based objections are waived. Plaintiffs must provide verified, objection-free responses to any of the discovery requests it has not already responded to within 10 days of the date of this order. Plaintiffs did not oppose the motion, but instead provided another list of excuses for not responding to the requests. The record does not show Plaintiff acted with substantial justification with respect to this discovery. Plaintiff and counsel, jointly and severally, shall pay $2,100 to Defendant in sanctions. Payment should be made no later than 10 court days from notice of this order. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the moving party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)

rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Friday, April 25, 2025, Line 6. 2 - DEFENDANT CRUISE LLC.'s Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Action. This matter will be specially set at 1:30 pm and the hearing will be conducted in Department 301. Counsel and parties may access the hearing remotely via the below Zoom links to Department 301. Defendant Cruise LLC's renewed motion to compel arbitration and to stay action is granted. The only ground asserted by the plaintiff to oppose this motion is that plaintiff's sex and pregnancy harassment claims are not arbitrable. However, that ground is foreclosed by the February 20, 2025 order granting judgment on the pleadings without leave to amend as to plaintiff's sex and pregnancy harassment claims and the ruling denying plaintiff's motion for reconsideration of the February 20, 2025 order. rulings.Case Title: YASMIN ABDULMALIK VS. CRUISE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Reconsideration For Court'S Order Granting In Part And Denying In Part Cruise'S Motion To Stay Pending Appeal And Alternative Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings Per Code Of Civil Procedure Section 1008(A); Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Mahru Madjidi; Exhibits
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Friday, April 25, 2025, Line 6. 1 - PLAINTIFF YASMIN ABDULMALIK's Motion For Reconsideration For Court'S Order Granting In Part And Denying In Part Cruise'S Motion To Stay Pending Appeal And Alternative Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings Per Code Of Civil Procedure Section 1008(A). This matter will be specially set at 1:30 pm and the hearing will be conducted in Department 301. Counsel and parties may access the hearing remotely via the below Zoom links to Department 301. Plaintiff's motion for reconsideration of the February 20, 2025 order granting judgment on the pleadings without leave to amend as to plaintiff's sex and pregnancy harassment claims is denied. Even assuming that plaintiff has shown new or different facts or circumstances that properly invoke the reconsideration procedure of CCP 1008, which is doubtful at best, the court remains of the view that the motion for judgment on the pleading was properly granted without leave to amend as to plaintiff's sex and pregnancy harassment claims. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Preliminary Approval Of Class Action And Paga Settlement
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Friday, April 25, 2025, Line 10. PLAINTIFF SANTOS QUINTAL's Motion For Preliminary Approval Of Class Action And Paga Settlement. Plaintiff Santoa Quintal's unopposed Motion For Preliminary Approval Of Class Action And PAGA Settlement is GRANTED. Good cause appearing, the court: (1) grants preliminary approval of the proposed Class Action and PAGA Settlement Agreement ("Settlement"); (2) conditionally certifies the proposed Class for settlement purposes only; (3) appoints Plaintiff as the Class Representative; (4) appoints Haulk & Herrera LLP as Class Counsel; (5) appoints ILYM to serve as the Settlement Administrator; (6) approves the use of the proposed notice procedure and related notice form; (7) directs that notice be sent to the Settlement Class; and (8) sets a final approval hearing date for November 17, 2025 at 9:00 am in this department, for which Plaintiff shall file a memorandum identifying and discussing outstanding issues no later than 10 court days before the hearing, with courtesy copy delivered to the department within 24 hours of filing. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the moving party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)
